frameworks.name: latent-state generative model with reward-gated modulation
frameworks.one_line: reward outcome gates how the latent environment model influences the current choice

frameworks.name: sequential-sampling transition bias
frameworks.one_line: trial-to-trial repeat/alternate bias extended with an outcome-dependent gate

frameworks.name: reinforcement-learning style outcome modulation
frameworks.one_line: previous-trial reward signal modulates use of internal expectations

core_question: Can the impact of expectations on perceptual decisions be flexibly modulated by the outcome of the previous decision?
key_assumptions: subjects maintain an internal estimate of stimulus-sequence repeating probability
expectations and sensory evidence combine to drive choice
previous-trial outcome can act as a modulatory gate without resetting the internal estimate
manipulations: block-wise repeating probability of previous stimulus category
trial position relative to error and subsequent correct
key_findings: rats exploit block-wise sequence correlations via a transition bias
the transition bias is null on trials following an error
the bias re-emerges after the next correct response, indicating the internal estimate is not reset
interpretations: a reward-driven modulatory signal gates the impact of the latent environmental model on current decisions
expectations and their deployment are dissociable: storage of the model is preserved while its influence is gated by recent outcomes
